prompt: Turn on the livingroom light now.
eval_note:
Expected decision: directly set Livingroom led1 = true (espID 1, actuator, led1, set, true).
A good answer should execute the light change and clearly inform the user that the action was made.
No unrelated tools or extra appliance changes should be used.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Turn on the livingroom light now
    Agent->>House: set espID1 led1 = true
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm livingroom light turned on

prompt: Set the hallway fan to 65%.
eval_note:
Expected decision: directly set Hallway motor1 = 65 (espID 2).
The agent should not modify hallway lights or door.
A correct result includes an execution confirmation.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Set hallway fan to 65%
    Agent->>House: set espID2 motor1 = 65
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm hallway fan set to 65%

prompt: What is the bedroom temperature right now?
eval_note:
Expected decision: get Bedroom temperature sensor only (espID 3, sensor, tem, get).
The agent should report the retrieved value and should not change any appliance.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: What is the bedroom temperature right now?
    Agent->>House: get espID3 tem
    House-->>Agent: current bedroom temperature
    Agent-->>Owner: report value with no execution

prompt: I'm about to arrive home. Please open the main door, turn on both hallway lights, and turn on the livingroom light.
eval_note:
Expected decision: set hallway door motor2 to an open position, set hallway led1 = true, hallway led2 = true, and livingroom led1 = true.
Because the request says "about to arrive home" and implies immediate action, direct execution is acceptable.
The agent should not touch bedroom appliances.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Prepare arrival home
    Agent->>House: set espID2 motor2 = open value
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led1 = true
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led2 = true
    Agent->>House: set espID1 led1 = true
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm door and lights prepared

prompt: I'm leaving the house now. Turn off all lights, stop all fans, close the main door, close the bedroom curtain, and lock the bedroom lock.
eval_note:
Expected decision: turn off led1 in livingroom; hallway led1 and led2 off; bedroom led1, led2, led3 off; motor1 fans to 0 in all rooms; hallway motor2 to fully closed; bedroom motor2 to fully closed; bedroom servo to locked state.
The agent should provide a concise confirmation of all executed changes.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Leaving house now
    Agent->>House: set all lights off
    Agent->>House: set all fans to 0
    Agent->>House: set hallway main door closed
    Agent->>House: set bedroom curtain closed
    Agent->>House: set bedroom lock locked
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm away-mode actions

prompt: It's really hot in the bedroom. Make it more comfortable for me.
eval_note:
Expected decision: interpret comfort intent and improve bedroom conditions.
Reasonable action: set bedroom fan to a moderate value such as 50-80; optionally check bedroom temperature first before deciding.
The agent should avoid unrelated appliances and should explain what it changed.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: It's really hot in the bedroom
    Agent->>House: get espID3 tem
    House-->>Agent: temperature
    Agent->>House: set espID3 motor1 = comfortable speed
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: explain comfort adjustment

prompt: I'm scared walking through the hallway at night, please make it easier for me.
eval_note:
Expected decision: infer that hallway lighting should be enabled.
A good answer turns on hallway lights and may optionally turn on livingroom light if helpful, but should avoid random fan or door changes.
The answer should acknowledge the user's feeling and state what was done.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: I'm scared walking through the hallway at night
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led1 = true
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led2 = true
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: reassure and confirm brighter hallway

prompt: I want movie mode in the livingroom. First check the livingroom temperature. If it is above 27°C, set the fan to 55%. Then turn off the livingroom light.
eval_note:
Expected decision: first get livingroom temperature sensor (espID 1, tem).
If temperature > 27, set livingroom fan motor1 = 55, then set livingroom light led1 = false.
If temperature <= 27, only turn off the light.
The order matters: sensor read before conditional fan control.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Activate livingroom movie mode
    Agent->>House: get espID1 tem
    House-->>Agent: livingroom temperature
    alt temp > 27
        Agent->>House: set espID1 motor1 = 55
    end
    Agent->>House: set espID1 led1 = false
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: report actions based on temperature

prompt: I am going to sleep in the bedroom now. Check the bedroom temperature first. If it is above 26.5°C, turn on the bedroom fan to 45%. Turn off the room light and bed light, close the curtain, and lock the bedroom.
eval_note:
Expected decision: get espID3 tem first.
If above threshold, set bedroom fan motor1 = 45; then set led1=false, led2=false, motor2=0 (closed curtain), servo to locked state.
The agent should not change balcony light unless the user asked.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Prepare bedroom for sleep
    Agent->>House: get espID3 tem
    House-->>Agent: bedroom temperature
    alt temp > 26.5
        Agent->>House: set espID3 motor1 = 45
    end
    Agent->>House: set espID3 led1 = false
    Agent->>House: set espID3 led2 = false
    Agent->>House: set espID3 motor2 = closed
    Agent->>House: set espID3 servo = locked
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm bedroom sleep setup

prompt: I will arrive home in 20 minutes. Please open the main door and turn on both hallway lights when I arrive.
eval_note:
Expected decision: use trigger_schedule_agent, not immediate appliance execution.
The request is future-oriented and should be translated into a scheduled action for the hallway door and lights around arrival time.
A strong answer clearly says the schedule was created.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant Scheduler
    Owner->>Agent: I will arrive home in 20 minutes
    Agent->>Scheduler: create scheduled arrival action
    Scheduler-->>Agent: schedule saved
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm scheduled door and hallway lights

prompt: I am going to nap in the bedroom for about 30 minutes. Please turn off the room light, set the bedroom fan to 40%, and after 30 minutes turn the fan off again.
eval_note:
Expected decision: use trigger_schedule_agent because the request includes timed behavior and reversal.
A correct interpretation schedules immediate sleep-state actions and a later restore or turn-off action after 30 minutes.
Immediate one-shot control without scheduling is incomplete.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant Scheduler
    Owner->>Agent: Nap for 30 minutes
    Agent->>Scheduler: create timed bedroom routine with reverse step
    Scheduler-->>Agent: schedule saved
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm timed nap routine

prompt: Add a note for 2026-04-07: 'Remember to clean the bonsai area and check the balcony pump.'
eval_note:
Expected decision: use add_note with the given date.
No appliance control should happen.
The response should mention that the note was stored successfully.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant Notes
    Owner->>Agent: Add note for 2026-04-07
    Agent->>Notes: add_note(...)
    Notes-->>Agent: note id created
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm note saved

prompt: Read all notes for 2026-04-07.
eval_note:
Expected decision: use read_note with date 2026-04-07.
The agent should return the notes and avoid appliance execution.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant Notes
    Owner->>Agent: Read notes for 2026-04-07
    Agent->>Notes: read_note(date=2026-04-07)
    Notes-->>Agent: notes list
    Agent-->>Owner: report notes

prompt: Check how many notes I have on 2026-04-07.
eval_note:
Expected decision: use check_note with the requested date.
The agent should report note count and note IDs if available.
No appliance action is needed.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant Notes
    Owner->>Agent: Check notes on 2026-04-07
    Agent->>Notes: check_note(date=2026-04-07)
    Notes-->>Agent: count and ids
    Agent-->>Owner: report count

eval_note:
Expected decision: use delete_note with the given note ID.
No appliance action should be taken.
The agent should confirm deletion result.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant Notes
    Owner->>Agent: Delete a note by id
    Agent->>Notes: delete_note(id)
    Notes-->>Agent: deletion result
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm deletion

prompt:
It is 2026-04-06 10:00:00, no event or execution reached.
Please provide your suggestions or have a general check of the house appliances system and take action if necessary.

Current house state:
Livingroom:
- Light off
- Fan 0%
- PIR: no motion
- Temperature: 25.6°C

Hallway:
- Left light off
- Right light off
- Fan 0%
- Main door 0% fully closed
- PIR: no motion
- Temperature: 26.4°C

Bedroom + balcony:
- Room light off
- Bed light off
- Balcony light off
- Fan 0%
- Window curtain 0% fully closed
- Lock locked
- Pump off
- PIR: no motion
- Temperature: 27.1°C
- Outside temperature: 27.8°C
- Moisture: 45%
eval_note:
Expected decision: overall state is normal for an idle house.
Best result is either no action or a minimal suggestion only. The agent should avoid unnecessary executions.
A poor result would randomly turn devices on without evidence.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Scheduler
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Scheduler->>Agent: No moment reached + current state
    Agent->>House: optional status verification
    House-->>Agent: status consistent
    Agent-->>Scheduler: no action needed or only minor suggestion

It is 2026-04-06 13:40:00, no event or execution reached.
Please provide your suggestions or have a general check of the house appliances system and take action if necessary.

Current house state:
Livingroom:
- Light off
- Fan 0%
- PIR: no motion
- Temperature: 26.0°C

Hallway:
- Left light off
- Right light off
- Fan 0%
- Main door 70% open
- PIR: no motion
- Temperature: 26.2°C

Bedroom + balcony:
- Room light off
- Bed light off
- Balcony light off
- Fan 0%
- Window curtain 0% fully closed
- Lock unlocked
- Pump off
- PIR: no motion

eval_note:
Expected decision: this idle-house snapshot has security anomalies: hallway main door is open and bedroom lock is unlocked while there is no motion anywhere.
Reasonable action: close the main door and lock the bedroom; the agent should explain the security-based decision.
No need to change lights or fans.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Scheduler
    participant Agent

    Scheduler->>Agent: Overall idle check with open door and unlocked lock

prompt:
It is 2026-04-06 06:00:00, too early and have not reached any events or execution yet.
Have a general check of the house appliances system and compare it with the initial settings below:
{
  "livingroom": {"light": false, "fan": 0},
  "hallway": {"left_light": false, "right_light": false, "fan": 0, "main_door": 0},
  "bedroom": {"room_light": false, "bed_light": false, "balcony_light": false, "fan": 0, "curtain": 0, "lock": true, "pump": false}
}

Current observed state:
- Livingroom light = true
- Hallway left light = false
- Hallway right light = false
- Hallway fan = 0
- Main door = 0
- Bedroom room light = false
- Bed light = true
- Balcony light = false
- Bedroom fan = 0
- Curtain = 0
- Lock = false
- Pump = false
eval_note:
Expected decision: compare current state with init baseline and correct mismatches.
At minimum: turn off livingroom light, turn off bed light, and lock bedroom.
The agent should not modify already-correct devices.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Scheduler
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Scheduler->>Agent: Init check with baseline and observed mismatch
    Agent->>House: set espID1 led1 = false
    Agent->>House: set espID3 led2 = false
    Agent->>House: set espID3 servo = locked
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Scheduler: report init-state corrections

prompt:
It is 2026-04-06 09:30:00, here is owner's activity period:
[
  {
    "type": "activity",
    "data": {
      "name": "working_from_home_in_livingroom",
      "expected_duration": "09:00-12:00",
      "note": "Owner is usually in the livingroom during this period."
    }
  }
]
Have a general check of the house appliances system to ensure the appliances are set up correctly according to the owner's activity, take action if necessary.

Current house state:
- Livingroom light off
- Livingroom fan 0%
- Livingroom PIR motion detected
- Livingroom temperature 29.2°C
- Hallway all lights off, fan 0, door closed, no motion
- Bedroom all lights off, fan 0, curtain closed, lock unlocked, no motion
eval_note:
Expected decision: infer that livingroom should be comfortable for active occupancy because PIR confirms presence and temperature is high.
Reasonable action: turn on livingroom light if lighting is needed and/or set livingroom fan to a comfort level.
The strongest grounded action here is increasing livingroom fan because temperature is 29.2°C.
The agent should not overreact to the unlocked bedroom if the owner is at home unless it explicitly justifies a security preference.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Scheduler
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Scheduler->>Agent: Working-from-home moment + current state
    Agent->>House: set espID1 motor1 = comfort speed
    opt lighting support
        Agent->>House: set espID1 led1 = true
    end
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Scheduler: report comfort-focused adjustment

eval_note:
Expected decision: execute the scheduled appliance settings directly because no special permission note is present.
The agent should set hallway led1=true, hallway led2=true, and livingroom led1=true, then confirm execution.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Scheduler
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Scheduler->>Agent: appliance_setting moment reached
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led1 = true
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led2 = true
    Agent->>House: set espID1 led1 = true
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Scheduler: confirm scheduled lights applied

eval_note:
Expected decision: because user confirmation is explicitly included and positive, the agent should execute the requested security actions.
A correct result closes hallway main door and locks the bedroom, then confirms execution.
If confirmation were missing or negative, execution should not happen.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Scheduler
    participant Agent
    participant User
    participant House
    Scheduler->>Agent: permission-required moment
    User-->>Agent: Yes, do it now
    Agent->>House: set espID3 servo = locked
    Agent->>House: set espID2 motor2 = closed
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Scheduler: confirm permissioned execution

prompt: Set the hallway fan to 150%.
eval_note:
Expected decision: reject or correct the out-of-range value because hallway fan accepts only 0-100.
Best behavior: explain valid range and either ask for a valid value or clamp only if the policy allows explicit safe correction.
Blindly sending value 150 is incorrect.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    Owner->>Agent: Set hallway fan to 150%
    Agent-->>Owner: explain valid range is 0-100 and refuse/ask correction

prompt: Open the bedroom curtain to -10%.
eval_note:
Expected decision: reject or correct because curtain motor2 value must be 0-100.
The agent should not execute a negative value.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    Owner->>Agent: Set curtain to -10%
    Agent-->>Owner: explain invalid range and refuse/ask correction

prompt: Turn on the kitchen light.
eval_note:
Expected decision: kitchen does not exist in the known house areas and appliance list.
The agent should explain that the target area/device is unavailable and should not fabricate a tool call or house action.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    Owner->>Agent: Turn on the kitchen light
    Agent-->>Owner: explain kitchen is not in available house map

prompt: Turn on the hallway left light and turn it off at the same time.
eval_note:
Expected decision: identify conflicting instructions and ask for clarification or state that the request is contradictory.
It should not randomly choose one action without explanation.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    Owner->>Agent: Conflicting light command
    Agent-->>Owner: explain conflict and ask for a single final state

prompt: The bedroom feels warm. Check the bedroom temperature and outside temperature. If outside is cooler than inside by at least 1°C, open the bedroom curtain to 100% and keep fan at 0%. Otherwise set the bedroom fan to 50%.
eval_note:
Expected decision: get espID3 tem and espID3 tem_out first.
If tem_out <= tem - 1.0, open curtain fully and keep or set fan to 0.
Otherwise set bedroom fan to 50.
This is a multi-sensor conditional decision test.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Compare inside and outside bedroom conditions
    Agent->>House: get espID3 tem
    Agent->>House: get espID3 tem_out
    House-->>Agent: inside and outside temperatures
    alt outside cooler by at least 1C
        Agent->>House: set espID3 motor2 = 100
        Agent->>House: set espID3 motor1 = 0
    else not cooler enough
        Agent->>House: set espID3 motor1 = 50
    end
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: report decision

prompt: Should I water the bonsai today? Please check the outside moisture and today's hourly forecast first, then decide whether to turn on the pump.
eval_note:
Expected decision: use moisture sensor (espID 3, mois) and weather forecast tool before deciding.
Good logic: if moisture is already high or rain is likely soon, avoid watering; if moisture is low and no rain is expected soon, pump may be turned on.
The answer should explain the reasoning and only then execute pump if justified.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    participant Weather
    Owner->>Agent: Decide whether to water bonsai today
    Agent->>House: get espID3 mois
    Agent->>Weather: get hourly forecast
    House-->>Agent: moisture value
    Weather-->>Agent: forecast data
    alt dry enough and low rain chance
        Agent->>House: set espID3 pump = true
        House-->>Agent: success
    else not needed
        Agent-->>Owner: recommend no watering now
    end

prompt: I want fresh air in the bedroom this afternoon. Check today's weather first. If rain is expected in the next 2 hours, keep the curtain closed. Otherwise open the curtain to 100%.
eval_note:
Expected decision: use hourly forecast tool first, then control bedroom curtain motor2 accordingly.
The agent should not rely only on the current indoor temperature because the condition is explicitly weather-based.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant Weather
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Fresh air decision for bedroom
    Agent->>Weather: get hourly forecast
    Weather-->>Agent: rain outlook
    alt rain expected soon
        Agent->>House: keep espID3 motor2 = 0 or do nothing if already closed
    else no rain expected
        Agent->>House: set espID3 motor2 = 100
    end
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: explain weather-based choice

prompt:
Emergency check:
Time: 2026-04-06 02:10:00
Current house state:
- Hallway main door 100% open
- Hallway PIR motion detected
- Livingroom PIR no motion
- Bedroom PIR no motion
- All hallway lights off

Please decide what to do.
eval_note:
Expected decision: treat as suspicious late-night security situation.
Reasonable actions: turn on hallway lights immediately and inform the user about unexpected motion/open door; optionally suggest caution rather than silently ignoring it.
The agent should avoid unlocking or opening more devices.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Monitor
    participant Agent
    participant House
    participant User
    Monitor->>Agent: Night anomaly with open door and motion
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led1 = true
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led2 = true
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>User: alert suspicious hallway activity

prompt:
Emergency-like anomaly:
Time: 2026-04-06 14:05:00
No motion detected in any room.
Livingroom temperature suddenly reads 39.8°C.
Hallway temperature 26.0°C.
Bedroom temperature 27.1°C.

Please decide what to do.
eval_note:
Expected decision: detect abnormal local temperature spike in livingroom and respond cautiously.
Reasonable action: turn off livingroom light if it is on, keep fan available for ventilation if appropriate, and alert/report anomaly rather than pretending everything is normal.
Because there is no dedicated smoke sensor, the evaluation should focus on anomaly recognition and cautious response, not on a single mandatory actuator pattern.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Monitor
    participant Agent
    participant House
    participant User
    Monitor->>Agent: Abnormal livingroom temperature spike
    Agent->>House: optional status checks
    Agent-->>User: warn about unusual high temperature
    opt reduce heat sources or ventilate
        Agent->>House: adjust livingroom appliances safely
        House-->>Agent: success
    end

prompt:
General check:
- Balcony pump has been on for 45 minutes continuously.
- Outside moisture is already 82%.
- No one is on the balcony.

Please decide what to do.
eval_note:
Expected decision: stop the pump because continuous watering with already high moisture is wasteful and potentially harmful.
The agent should set espID3 pump = false and explain why.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Monitor
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Monitor->>Agent: Pump on too long, moisture already high
    Agent->>House: set espID3 pump = false
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>User: report overwatering prevention

prompt:
Security anomaly:
- Main door is 100% open
- Hallway PIR motion detected
- Bedroom lock is unlocked
- Owner has not requested any arrival action

Please decide what to do.
eval_note:
Expected decision: recognize security risk.
A grounded response should at least alert the user and turn on hallway lights; it may also recommend locking the bedroom after the situation is clarified.
The agent should not auto-lock the main exit while motion is present unless it explains safety reasoning carefully.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Monitor
    participant Agent
    participant House
    participant User
    Monitor->>Agent: Open door + motion + unlocked bedroom
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led1 = true
    Agent->>House: set espID2 led2 = true
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>User: alert security anomaly and recommend action

prompt:
It is 2026-04-06 12:30:00, no event or execution reached.
Please provide your suggestions or have a general check of the house appliances system and take action if necessary.

Current house state:
Livingroom:
- Light on

- Left light on

Bedroom + balcony:
- Room light on
- Bed light off
- Balcony light on
- Fan 80%
- Curtain fully closed

Expected decision: idle house with many devices left on suggests energy waste.
Reasonable action: turn off unused lights, turn all fans to 0 because there is no motion anywhere and temperatures are moderate, and optionally lock the bedroom for security.

sequenceDiagram

    participant Agent
    participant House
    Scheduler->>Agent: Idle-house energy waste snapshot

    opt security preference

    end
    House-->>Agent: success

prompt:
It is 2026-04-06 15:20:00, no event or execution reached.
Please provide your suggestions or have a general check of the house appliances system and take action if necessary.

Current house state:
- No motion in livingroom, hallway, or bedroom
- Hallway main door fully closed
- Bedroom lock unlocked
- All lights off
- All fans 0
- Curtain 0 fully closed
- Pump off
eval_note:
Expected decision: overall state is mostly good; the only likely security improvement is locking the bedroom.
A good result either locks the bedroom or explicitly suggests doing so; it should not invent extra actions.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Scheduler
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Scheduler->>Agent: Mostly normal idle house except unlocked bedroom
    Agent->>House: set espID3 servo = locked
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Scheduler: report minor security correction

prompt: I'm going to read in bed for the next hour. Make the bedroom comfortable for reading.
eval_note:
Expected decision: interpret reading context.
Reasonable action: turn on bed light, optionally keep room light off or on depending on preference, and use fan only if needed after checking or inferring comfort.
A minimal good answer is bed light on with a clear confirmation.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Prepare bedroom for reading
    Agent->>House: set espID3 led2 = true
    opt comfort adjustment
        Agent->>House: get espID3 tem
        Agent->>House: set espID3 motor1 = comfortable speed
    end
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm reading setup

prompt: I have a guest coming in five minutes, make the house welcoming.
eval_note:
Expected decision: because the request is short-future and somewhat vague, two acceptable strategies exist:
1) immediate prep if interpreted as "prepare now", or
2) use trigger_schedule_agent for actions in five minutes.
Strong grounded prep includes hallway lights on, livingroom light on, and possibly opening the main door at arrival time rather than immediately.
The evaluation should reward coherent interpretation and minimal, relevant actions.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant Scheduler
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Guest coming in five minutes
    alt immediate preparation
        Agent->>House: turn on welcoming lights
        House-->>Agent: success
    else schedule at arrival time
        Agent->>Scheduler: create guest-arrival action
        Scheduler-->>Agent: schedule saved
    end
    Agent-->>Owner: explain chosen preparation plan

prompt: Good morning. Open the bedroom curtain, turn on the livingroom light, and set the hallway fan to 30%.
eval_note:
Expected decision: set bedroom curtain motor2 = 100, livingroom led1 = true, hallway motor1 = 30.
The agent should confirm all three actions and avoid changing unrelated bedroom lights or lock.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Good morning routine
    Agent->>House: set espID3 motor2 = 100
    Agent->>House: set espID1 led1 = true
    Agent->>House: set espID2 motor1 = 30
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm morning setup

prompt: Good night. Turn off every light, stop all fans, close the main door, close the bedroom curtain, and lock the bedroom.
eval_note:
Expected decision: execute a whole-house night mode similar to away/sleep security state.
The agent should switch all lights off, all fans to 0, main door closed, bedroom curtain closed, bedroom lock locked.

    participant Agent

    Agent->>House: all fans 0

    Agent->>House: curtain closed
    Agent->>House: bedroom locked
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm night mode

prompt: Add a note for 2026-04-08 saying 'Parents visiting at 19:00'. Also, please schedule the hallway lights and livingroom light to turn on at 18:55 that day.
eval_note:
Expected decision: this combines note storage and future scheduling.
The agent should use add_note for the date and trigger_schedule_agent for the future light actions.
Doing only one of the two is incomplete.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant Notes
    participant Scheduler
    Owner->>Agent: Add visit note and light schedule
    Agent->>Notes: add_note(...)
    Notes-->>Agent: note saved
    Agent->>Scheduler: create 2026-04-08 18:55 light schedule
    Scheduler-->>Agent: schedule saved
    Agent-->>Owner: confirm both note and schedule

prompt: It is 2026-04-08 18:50:00. Before deciding any preparation, check whether there are notes for today. If there is a note saying guests or parents are visiting, prepare the hallway and livingroom lights accordingly.
eval_note:
Expected decision: use note checking/reading first, then conditionally execute or schedule relevant light preparation.
This tests whether the agent can use notes as context for house behavior.
A correct answer should not ignore the note tool.

    participant Agent

    Agent->>Notes: check/read note for 2026-04-08
    Notes-->>Agent: visit note present
    Agent->>House: set hallway and livingroom lights on
    House-->>Agent: success
    Agent-->>Scheduler: report note-driven preparation

prompt: What time is it now?
eval_note:
Expected decision: use get_current_datetime.
The agent should return the current date/time and must not control appliances.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant TimeTool
    Owner->>Agent: What time is it now?
    Agent->>TimeTool: get_current_datetime
    TimeTool-->>Agent: current date and time

prompt: Check the balcony moisture sensor for me.
eval_note:
Expected decision: get espID3 moisture sensor only.
No appliance should be changed.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Check balcony moisture
    Agent->>House: get espID3 mois
    House-->>Agent: moisture value
    Agent-->>Owner: report moisture

prompt: Please check the livingroom temperature. If it is 28°C or higher, set the livingroom fan to 60%. Otherwise leave it off.
eval_note:
Expected decision: get livingroom tem first, then conditionally set livingroom fan.
The agent should not guess the temperature without reading it.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Conditional livingroom fan
    Agent->>House: get espID1 tem
    House-->>Agent: temperature
    alt temp >= 28
        Agent->>House: set espID1 motor1 = 60
        House-->>Agent: success
    else temp < 28
        Agent-->>Owner: leave fan off
    end

prompt: Check the balcony moisture first. If it is below 30%, turn on the pump. If it is 30% or above, do not water.
eval_note:
Expected decision: read espID3 mois first, then conditionally set pump.
The agent should not water without a sensor-based decision because the rule is explicit.
ground_truth_mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Owner
    participant Agent
    participant House
    Owner->>Agent: Moisture-based watering rule
    Agent->>House: get espID3 mois
    House-->>Agent: moisture value
    alt moisture < 30
        Agent->>House: set espID3 pump = true
        House-->>Agent: success
    else moisture >= 30
        Agent-->>Owner: no watering needed
    end

eval_note:
Expected decision: this is a mixed anomaly case. With no motion anywhere, the house has energy waste and security issues.
Strong actions: turn off livingroom light, bed light, balcony light; set livingroom and bedroom fans to 0; close main door; lock bedroom; turn off pump because moisture is already high; consider closing curtain as part of night/security cleanup.
